what_it_does: This file is agent-facing documentation for the src/feedback/ subsystem, describing the 'teach Vi' annotation/feedback loop, the files that implement it, and the conventions for adding or extending feedback behavior.
observations: The subsystem is called the 'teach Vi' annotation / feedback loop.
Four files are described: feedback-actions.js (window action registry), feedback-store.js (state/store), feedback-ui.jsx (interaction surface), feedback-memory.jsx (rendering learned guidance).
Components opt into feedback via window.useFeedback({...}) and spread fb.target(descriptor) onto elements.
All feedback is routed through the action registry and store; no inline feedback state is allowed.
Colour/space/type use tokens; status uses MS_STATUS; no raw hex or px values.
The subsystem is currently wired into intake, with extension into the studio noted as a future thread.
standouts: The 'teach Vi' loop lets users comment on, redo, or pin guidance to elements.
Everything routes through a single action registry + store — one source of truth for what actions mean and where feedback lives.
When extending into the studio, the doc explicitly says to reuse this registry/store rather than fork it.
conventions: Centralized action registry and store for all feedback semantics.
No inline feedback state at call sites.
Components opt in via window.useFeedback and fb.target(descriptor).
Tokens for colour/space/type; status communicated via MS_STATUS.
No raw hex or px values.
concerns: The boundary between the current intake wiring and the future studio extension is mentioned but not specified in this file.
notes: This is the agent readme for the feedback/teach-Vi subsystem, mapping its four modules and the central registry/store convention.
questions: What does a target descriptor contain?
How are actions registered in feedback-actions.js?
What is MS_STATUS and where is it defined?
What token system is used for colour/space/type?
How is Vi memory persisted and loaded?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states it documents the feedback/teach-Vi subsystem and then does so.
apparent_themes: Centralized source of truth for actions and state.
Teachable assistant via user annotations and pinned guidance.
Reuse over duplication when extending scope.
intention_signals: Establish a maintainable, centralized feedback loop that lets users teach Vi by annotating elements, with the same registry/store reused across product surfaces.
novelty: The 'teach Vi' framing treats user feedback as persistent guidance anchors and anti-patterns, backed by a single action registry/store rather than per-surface logic.
contribution: Documents the feedback subsystem's module boundaries and conventions, preserving the centralized registry/store pattern and the 'teach Vi' annotation loop as a reusable integration point.
summary_for_embedding: Agent documentation for the src/feedback/ 'teach Vi' subsystem, listing the action registry, store, UI surface, and memory renderer plus conventions for tokens, status, and centralized state.
intention_for_embedding: To orient agents to the feedback/teach-Vi loop and enforce a single registry/store pattern when adding or extending feedback behavior.
